other_observers: Rob Sparks and Arvind Panjabi, both with RMBO.; Rob Sparks spotted and IDed the bird first.
specific_location: Soapstone Ranch just west of I-25 on the boarder of Wyoming.
description: Bright red head and throat. Long grayish sharp bill. Black back and tail. White wing patches on secondaries and white rump. White belly and breast.Behaviors: It was usually sitting vertically on tree branches and trunk. It would never let anyone get lose without flying away. It would occasionally sit in the open vertically on wooden fench posts. When I was digiscoping it, it would fly like it was flycatching up into the air, then come back and land back on the post.Call: Didn't hear itPlumage: Adult
